properties.remark: WIRES, UTILITY POLES AND TREES DOWN IN THE NACHES AND TIETON AREAS.
BRANCHES BREAKING OFF TREES AND BLOWING DUST REDUCING VISIBILITY TO HALF MILE AT TIMES. GARBAGE CANS AND ITEMS BEING THROWN INTO THE AIR. ESTIMATED WIND GUST TO 55 MPH.

A STATE TROOPER REPORTED THAT AREAS OF ZERO VISIBIBILITY DUE TO BLOWING DUST HAVE SPAWNED A COUPLE OF AUTO ACCIDENTS ALONG THE HIGHWAY 240 AND SR 24 NEAR VERNITA BRIDGE AND THE NORTHERN ENTRANCE OF HANFORD NUCLEAR RESERVATION. ROAD CLOSURES HAVE ALSO BEEN REPORTED IN THESE AREAS AND FROM MILE POST 50-70 ON INTERSTATE 90.
VISIBILITY REDUCED TO LESS THAN 100 FT IN BLOWING DUST ON INTERSTATE 84 MILEPOST 186 BETWEEN PENDLETON AND HERMISTON.
POWER OUTAGE DUE TO TREE BRANCHES DOWN ON POWER LINE. 20 FOOT TALL LILAC TREE UPROOTED AND SHINGLES TORN OFF ROOFS.
WIND DRIVEN HAIL UP TO GOLF BALL SIZE PUNCHED HOLES IN VINYL SIDING. OCCURRED JUST SOUTH OF TORNADO TRACK.

NWS TEAM DETERMINED THAT A TORNADO OF EF1 INTENSITY WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 1.6 MILES AND PATH WIDTH OF 40 YARDS OCCURRED FROM 4 SE OF ADAMS TO 5 ESE OF ADAMS FROM 455 PM TO 5 PM PDT. 100 FOOT SECTION OF METAL ROOF FROM WORKSHOP TORN OFF AND LOFTED UP TO 1/2 MILE. 50 GALLON DRUMS THROWN UP TO 1/4 MILE. TREE UPROOTED AND LIMBS DOWN. WOODEN PLANK DRIVEN THROUGH FRAME BLDG.

RANCH REPORTS THUNDERSTORM WINDS TORE LARGE WORKSHOP IN HALF AND CARRIED ROOF 100 YARDS BEFORE DROPPING IT ONTO THE ROOF OF ANOTHER BUILDING. SEVERAL 55 GALLON DRUMS LIFTED AT LEAST 100 FEET INTO AIR BEFORE BEING DROPPED BACK TO GROUND. NICKEL SIZE HAIL ALSO REPORTED.
PILOT REPORTED GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL NEAR GROUND LEVEL OVER THE BLUE MOUNTAINS.
PING PONG BALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED.
POLICE REPORTED GOLFBALL SIZE HAIL IN WARM SPRINGS.
IN TERREBONNE, REPORTING VERY HEAVY RAIN AND HAIL UP TO 1 INCH IN DIAMETER. WINDS 15-20 MPH.
SPOTTER FROM MADRAS WAS IN TERREBONNE, REPORTING VERY HEAVY RAIN AND HAIL UP TO 1 INCH IN DIAMETER. WINDS 15-20 MPH.
STRONG WINDS KNOCKED DOWN 6-12 INCH DIAMETER TREES AND KNOCKED OUT POWER. PRODUCED PEA SIZED HAIL AND FLOODED STREETS.
VERY HEAVY RAIN IN THE PAST 5 MINUTES. SOUTH WIND GUSTS OVER 30 MPH. 2 INCH TREE BRANCHES DOWN. A POWER OUTAGE WAS REPORTED.
DROVE INTO PRINEVILLE AND THERE WAS WATER COVERING THE ROAD AT NORTHWEST LAMONTA AND SHARP ROAD.
GUSTY WINDS WITH A LOT OF BROKEN TREE LIMBS AND TREE DAMAGE. 1/2 INCH OF RAIN IN A SHORT PERIOD AND PONDING UP TO 6 INCHES AT THE ELEMENTARY SCHOOL.

BETWEEN 330 PM TO 340 PM STRONG WINDS BLEW SHINGLES OFF ROOFS, PATIO FURNITURE AROUND, AND PIECES OF PLYWOOD WERE ALSO REPORTED BLOWING AWAY. THE TEMPERATURE DROPPED FROM 87 DEGREES BEFORE THE STORM, TO 63 DEGREES AFTERWARDS.
TREES DOWNED BY HIGH WINDS FROM THUNDERSTORM...LARGE AMOUNTS OF HAIL AND HEAVY RAIN CAUSED LOCAL FLOODING THAT EXCEEDED CITYS DRAIN CAPACITY.

HIGH WINDS FROM THUNDERSTORM DOWNED SIX LARGE JUNIPER TREES...5 WERE UPROOTED FROM DRY GROUND AND 1 WAS SPLIT DOWN MIDDLE AND SNAPPED. OTHER TREES LOST LARGE LIMBS WITH DEBRIS FOUND IN ADJACENT HAY FIELD.

HEAVY WINDS BLEW DOWN TREES THAT ARE 4-6 INCHES IN DIAMETER OR LARGER. A 25 FOOT SECTION OF 1/8 INCH THICK STEEL PANEL WAS BLOWN OFF OF A COW SHELTER. THE OBSERVER WAS NAVIGATING ON AN ATV, AND WAS BLOWN OFF THE ROAD MULTIPLE TIMES.
FIRE STATION RECEIVED REPORTS OF TREES DOWNED FROM THUNDERSTORM WINDS IN TWO LOCATIONS OF TOWN OF REITH.
WINDS ESTIMATED AT 55-60 MPH RIPPED AWNINGS OFF OF TRAVEL TRAILERS, AND SNAPPED TREE LIMBS AT 6 INCHES OF DIAMETER.
HEAVY RAIN FROM THUNDERSTORMS CAUSED A WASHOUT ALONG LITTLE SHEEP CREEK HIGHWAY TO IMNAHA ALONG MILEPOST 22.
A CAMP HOST AT SPRING CREEK RV PARK MEASURED A 1 3/8 INCH PIECE OF HAIL JUST OFF THE FREEWAY.
THUNDERSTORM PRODUCING HAIL ONE INCH IN DIAMETER.
HAM RADIO OPERATOR REPORTED QUARTER-SIZED HAIL.
REPORTED THUNDERSTORM PRODUCING QUARTER-SIZED HAIL.
ESTIMATED WINDS 50-60 MPH SUSTAINED, AND A TREE HAD COLLAPSED ON TOP OF A VEHICLE.
HAIL UP TO THE SIZE OF QUARTERS IN DIAMETER WERE REPORTED ALONG WITH MINOR FLOODING DUE TO BRIEF HEAVY RAIN.
SUDDEN AND SUSTAINED WINDS FROM COLLAPSING SUPERCELL BROKE ALL THREE BOAT DOCKS OFF THE SHORE OF PELTON PARK RESERVOIR. EVERY TENT IN THE PARK WAS FLATTENED BY THE WIND.
TREE LIMBS 1 1/2 INCH IN DIAMETER BLOWN OFF TREES. ESTIMAGED WINDS 35 MPH WITH HIGHER GUSTS.
SEVERAL ESTIMATED WIND GUSTS TO 50 MPH OVER A PERIOD OF 20-30 MINUTES. 1 TO 2 INCHES DIAMETER TREE LIMBS BROKEN OFF, WITH TREES BENDING NEAR SNAPPING POINT.
6-8 INCH DIAMETER TREE BRANCH DOWN ACROSS POWERLINE. POWER OUT TO SW PART OF CITY.
WIND GUSTS TO 60 MPH WITH CONTINUOUS LIGHTNING.
2 INCH DIAMETER TREE LIMBS BLOWN DOWN.
1/2 INCH HAIL ALSO REPORTED.
HEAVY RAIN WITH SOME PONDING ON ROADS.
1 INCH DIAMETER HAIL, COVERING GROUND TO A DEPTH OF 1 INCH IN PLACES. SMALL LIMBS BROUGHT DOWN.
GROUND WHITE WITH 1 INCH DIAMETER HAIL. SMALL BRANCHES DOWN.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL. HEAVY RAIN FOR ALMOST AN HOUR BUT NO FLOODING.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED QUARTER SIZED HAIL AT THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 20 AND GOSNEY ROAD.
